text:
Period of transition and gradual reduction of the enteral nutrition solution.
Stopping the CDED
•  Perte de poids involontaire : recalcul des apports énergétiques et ajustement 
du soluté de nutrition entérale +/- ajustement des recommandations 
diététiques.
•  Poor adherence to the enteral nutrition solution: adjustment of the flavor or concentration to your preferences, monitoring of nutritional adequacy.
•  Poor adherence to mandatory foods: review of recipes and adjustment of support.
Adjustment of the CDED
•  Active Crohn's disease, confirmed by imaging and biological markers.
•  Consultation with the gastroenterologist and meeting with the whole team (gastroenterologist, dietitian, ...) for shared medical decision-making.
•  Verification of tolerance to the enteral nutrition solution and to mandatory foods, and monitoring of therapeutic adherence for 12 weeks.
Decision to start the CDED
•  Dietary assessment: anthropometry and eating habits.
•  Education on phase 1 of the CDED diet, including mandatory and allowed foods.
•  Clinical evaluation and prescription
